name: Uncle Bryan's Sunset Suratt Surf School
tagline: A born-and-raised North Shore uncle who teaches surfing the way it was meant to be learned — with aloha, patience, and deep ocean knowledge.
price: $$ — Group lesson ~$85, private ~$175 (2 hrs, board and rash guard included)
parking: Meet at Haleiwa Beach Park; free parking lot usually has space
hours: Daily 9 AM–5 PM; lessons at 9 AM, 11 AM, 1 PM, 3 PM
bestTime: Summer (May–Sep) for the most beginner-friendly conditions on the North Shore; 9 AM session for glassiest water
kamaaina: Kamaaina discount available with valid Hawaii ID
talkStory: Bryan Suratt grew up surfing Haleiwa and Sunset Beach and has been teaching since the 1990s. His school is the real deal — no fancy marketing, no hotel lobby, just an uncle with decades of wave knowledge who genuinely loves putting people on their first wave. Lessons happen at Puaena Point and Haleiwa Beach Park where the waves are forgiving and the vibe is local. Bryan's instructors are all North Shore locals who grew up in the water, and they teach ocean safety and respect alongside technique. You'll learn to read currents, understand wave sets, and position yourself — not just pop up and fall. Many local families send their keiki here for summer surf camps. The school also runs intermediate sessions at Chun's Reef for those ready to graduate from whitewater.
